216:   if (!database) {

218:     return { success: false, error: 'Database reference is required' };
219:   }
220:   db = database;

222:   const schemaResult = ensureSchema();
223:   if (!schemaResult.success) {
224:     return schemaResult;
225:   }

227:   cleanupExpiredSessions();
228: 
229:   // Set up periodic cleanup instead of only running once at init (P4-18).
230:   // Clear any existing interval first (in case of reinitializeDatabase).
231:   if (cleanupInterval) {
232:     clearRegisteredTimer(cleanupInterval);
233:   }
234:   cleanupInterval = registerInterval(() => {
235:     try {
236:       cleanupExpiredSessions();
237:     } catch (err: unknown) {
238:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
239:       console.warn(`[session-manager] Periodic cleanup failed: ${message}`);
240:     }
241:   }, CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS, { unref: true });
242: 
243:   // Run stale session cleanup on startup and set up hourly interval
244:   try {
245:     cleanupStaleSessions();
246:   } catch (err: unknown) {
247:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
248:     console.warn(`[session-manager] Initial stale session cleanup failed: ${message}`);
249:   }
250: 
251:   if (staleCleanupInterval) {
252:     clearRegisteredTimer(staleCleanupInterval);
253:   }
254:   staleCleanupInterval = registerInterval(() => {
255:     try {
256:       cleanupStaleSessions();
257:     } catch (err: unknown) {
258:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
259:       console.warn(`[session-manager] Periodic stale session cleanup failed: ${message}`);
260:     }
261:   }, STALE_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS, { unref: true });
262: 
263:   if (retentionSweepInterval) {
264:     clearRegisteredTimer(retentionSweepInterval);
265:     retentionSweepInterval = null;
266:   }
267:   if (RETENTION_SWEEP_ENABLED) {
268:     try {
269:       runMemoryRetentionSweep(database as unknown as BetterSqlite.Database, { dryRun: false });
270:     } catch (err: unknown) {
271:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
272:       console.warn(`[session-manager] Initial memory retention sweep failed: ${message}`);
273:     }

275:     retentionSweepInterval = registerInterval(() => {
276:       try {
277:         runMemoryRetentionSweep(database as unknown as BetterSqlite.Database, { dryRun: false });
278:       } catch (err: unknown) {
279:         const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);

281:       }

413: function resolveTrustedSession(
414:   requestedSessionId: string | null = null,
415:   scope: SessionIdentityScope = {},
416: ): TrustedSessionResolution {
417:   const normalizedSessionId = typeof requestedSessionId === 'string' && requestedSessionId.trim().length > 0
418:     ? requestedSessionId.trim()
419:     : null;
420: 
421:   if (!normalizedSessionId) {
422:     return {
423:       requestedSessionId: null,
424:       effectiveSessionId: crypto.randomUUID(),
425:       trusted: false,
426:     };
427:   }
428: 
429:   if (!isTrackedSession(normalizedSessionId)) {
430:     return {
431:       requestedSessionId: normalizedSessionId,
432:       effectiveSessionId: '',
433:       trusted: false,
434:       error: `sessionId "${normalizedSessionId}" does not match a server-managed session. Omit sessionId to start a new server-generated session and reuse the effectiveSessionId returned by the server.`,
435:     };
436:   }
437: 
438:   const storedIdentity = getSessionIdentityRecord(normalizedSessionId);
439:   if (!hasCorroboratedIdentity(storedIdentity)) {
440:     return {
441:       requestedSessionId: normalizedSessionId,
442:       effectiveSessionId: '',
443:       trusted: false,
444:       error: `sessionId "${normalizedSessionId}" is not bound to a corroborated server identity. Omit sessionId to start a new server-generated session and reuse the effectiveSessionId returned by the server.`,
445:     };
446:   }
447: 
448:   const mismatch = getIdentityMismatch(storedIdentity, scope);
449:   if (mismatch) {
450:     return {
451:       requestedSessionId: normalizedSessionId,
452:       effectiveSessionId: '',
453:       trusted: false,
454:       error: `sessionId "${normalizedSessionId}" is bound to a different ${mismatch}. Omit sessionId to start a new server-generated session and reuse the effectiveSessionId returned by the server.`,
455:     };
456:   }
457: 
458:   return {
459:     requestedSessionId: normalizedSessionId,
460:     effectiveSessionId: normalizedSessionId,
461:     trusted: true,
462:   };
463: }

506: function generateMemoryHash(memory: MemoryInput): string {
507:   if (!memory) {
508:     throw new Error('Memory object is required for hash generation');
509:   }
510: 
511:   let hashInput: string;
512: 
513:   if (memory.content_hash) {
514:     hashInput = memory.content_hash;
515:   } else if (memory.id !== undefined) {
516:     // Support both anchor_id (snake_case) and anchorId (camelCase) — callers may pass either form (P4-16).
517:     hashInput = `${memory.id}:${memory.anchor_id || memory.anchorId || ''}:${memory.file_path || ''}`;
518:   } else {
519:     hashInput = JSON.stringify({
520:       // Prefer anchor_id (canonical), fall back to anchorId for legacy callers (P4-16)
521:       anchor: memory.anchor_id || memory.anchorId,
522:       path: memory.file_path,
523:       title: memory.title,
524:     });
525:   }
526: 
527:   // Use 128-bit (32 hex chars) instead of
528:   // 64-bit (16 hex chars) to reduce collision probability.
529:   return crypto.createHash('sha256').update(hashInput).digest('hex').slice(0, 32);
530: }

536: function shouldSendMemory(sessionId: string, memory: MemoryInput | number): boolean {
537:   if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled) return true;
538:   if (!db) {
539:     const allow = SESSION_CONFIG.dbUnavailableMode === 'allow';
540:     console.warn(`[session-manager] Database not initialized. dbUnavailableMode=${SESSION_CONFIG.dbUnavailableMode}. ${allow ? 'Allowing' : 'Blocking'} memory.`);
541:     return allow;
542:   }
543:   if (!sessionId || typeof sessionId !== 'string') return true;
544: 
545:   try {
546:     const memoryObj: MemoryInput = typeof memory === 'number' ? { id: memory } : memory;
547:     const hash = generateMemoryHash(memoryObj);
548: 
549:     const stmt = db.prepare(`
550:       SELECT 1 FROM session_sent_memories
551:       WHERE session_id = ? AND memory_hash = ?
552:       LIMIT 1
553:     `);
554:     const exists = stmt.get(sessionId, hash);
555:     return !exists;
556:   } catch (error: unknown) {
557:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
558:     console.warn(`[session-manager] shouldSendMemory check failed: ${message}`);
559:     return SESSION_CONFIG.dbUnavailableMode === 'allow';
560:   }
561: }

563: function shouldSendMemoriesBatch(
564:   sessionId: string,
565:   memories: MemoryInput[],
566:   markAsSent: boolean = false
567: ): Map<number, boolean> {
568:   const result = new Map<number, boolean>();
569: 
570:   if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled || !sessionId || !Array.isArray(memories)) {
571:     memories.forEach((m) => {
572:       if (m.id != null) {
573:         result.set(m.id, true);
574:       }
575:     });
576:     return result;
577:   }
578: 
579:   if (!db) {
580:     const allow = SESSION_CONFIG.dbUnavailableMode === 'allow';
581:     console.warn(`[session-manager] Database not initialized for batch dedup. dbUnavailableMode=${SESSION_CONFIG.dbUnavailableMode}. ${allow ? 'Allowing' : 'Blocking'} batch.`);
582:     memories.forEach((m) => {
583:       if (m.id != null) {
584:         result.set(m.id, allow);
585:       }
586:     });
587:     return result;
588:   }
589: 
590:   try {
591:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
592:     const existingStmt = db.prepare(`
593:       SELECT memory_hash FROM session_sent_memories WHERE session_id = ?
594:     `);
595:     const insertStmt = db.prepare(`
596:       INSERT OR IGNORE INTO session_sent_memories (session_id, memory_hash, memory_id, sent_at)
597:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
598:     `);
599: 
600:     const evaluateBatch = () => {
601:       const existingRows = existingStmt.all(sessionId) as { memory_hash: string }[];
602:       const existingHashes = new Set(existingRows.map((r) => r.memory_hash));
603: 
604:       for (const memory of memories) {
605:         const hash = generateMemoryHash(memory);
606:         let shouldSend = !existingHashes.has(hash);
607:         if (shouldSend && markAsSent) {
608:           const insertResult = insertStmt.run(sessionId, hash, memory.id || null, now);
609:           shouldSend = insertResult.changes > 0;
610:         }
611:         if (shouldSend) {
612:           existingHashes.add(hash);
613:         }
614:         // Preserve first-occurrence decision for the same memory ID — prevents double-counting.
615:         if (memory.id != null && !result.has(memory.id)) {
616:           result.set(memory.id, shouldSend);
617:         }
618:       }
619: 
620:       if (markAsSent) {
621:         // Check + mark + cap enforcement stay in one transaction to avoid duplicate injection races.
622:         enforceEntryLimit(sessionId);
623:       }
624:     };
625: 
626:     if (markAsSent) {
627:       const inTransaction = (db as unknown as { inTransaction?: boolean }).inTransaction === true;
628:       if (inTransaction) {
629:         evaluateBatch();
630:       } else {
631:         db.exec('BEGIN IMMEDIATE');
632:         try {
633:           evaluateBatch();
634:           db.exec('COMMIT');
635:         } catch (transactionError: unknown) {
636:           try {
637:             db.exec('ROLLBACK');
638:           } catch {
639:             // Ignore rollback errors after failed transaction.
640:           }
641:           throw transactionError;
642:         }
643:       }
644:     } else {
645:       evaluateBatch();
646:     }
647: 
648:     return result;
649:   } catch (error: unknown) {
650:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
651:     console.warn(`[session-manager] shouldSendMemoriesBatch failed: ${message}`);
652:     const allow = SESSION_CONFIG.dbUnavailableMode === 'allow';
653:     memories.forEach((m) => {
654:       if (m.id != null) {
655:         result.set(m.id, allow);
656:       }
657:     });
658:     return result;
659:   }
660: }

662: function markMemorySent(sessionId: string, memory: MemoryInput | number): MarkResult {
663:   if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled) return { success: true, skipped: true };
664:   if (!db) return { success: false, error: 'Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.' };
665:   if (!sessionId || typeof sessionId !== 'string') {
666:     return { success: false, error: 'Valid sessionId is required' };
667:   }
668: 
669:   try {
670:     const memoryObj: MemoryInput = typeof memory === 'number' ? { id: memory } : memory;
671:     const hash = generateMemoryHash(memoryObj);
672:     const memoryId = memoryObj.id || null;
673: 
674:     const stmt = db.prepare(`
675:       INSERT OR IGNORE INTO session_sent_memories (session_id, memory_hash, memory_id, sent_at)
676:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
677:     `);
678: 
679:     // Transaction ensures atomic insert + limit enforcement, preventing concurrent race past entry limit.
680:     db.transaction(() => {
681:       stmt.run(sessionId, hash, memoryId, new Date().toISOString());
682:       // EnforceEntryLimit inside tx — atomic with insert to prevent row count races.
683:       enforceEntryLimit(sessionId);
684:     })();
685: 
686:     return { success: true, hash };
687:   } catch (error: unknown) {
688:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
689:     console.error(`[session-manager] markMemorySent failed: ${message}`);
690:     return { success: false, error: message };
691:   }
692: }

694: function markMemoriesSentBatch(sessionId: string, memories: MemoryInput[]): MarkBatchResult {
695:   if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled) return { success: true, markedCount: 0, skipped: true };
696:   if (!db) return { success: false, markedCount: 0, error: 'Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.' };
697:   if (!sessionId || !Array.isArray(memories) || memories.length === 0) {
698:     return { success: false, markedCount: 0, error: 'Valid sessionId and memories array required' };
699:   }
700: 
701:   try {
702:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
703:     let markedCount = 0;
704: 
705:     const insertStmt = db.prepare(`
706:       INSERT OR IGNORE INTO session_sent_memories (session_id, memory_hash, memory_id, sent_at)
707:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
708:     `);
709: 
710:     const runBatch = db.transaction(() => {
711:       for (const memory of memories) {
712:         const hash = generateMemoryHash(memory);
713:         const result = insertStmt.run(sessionId, hash, memory.id || null, now);
714:         if (result.changes > 0) {
715:           markedCount++;
716:         }
717:       }
718:       // EnforceEntryLimit inside tx — atomic with inserts to prevent row count races.
719:       enforceEntryLimit(sessionId);
720:     });
721: 
722:     runBatch();
723: 
724:     return { success: true, markedCount };
725:   } catch (error: unknown) {
726:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
727:     console.error(`[session-manager] markMemoriesSentBatch failed: ${message}`);
728:     return { success: false, markedCount: 0, error: message };
729:   }
730: }

736: function enforceEntryLimit(sessionId: string): void {
737:   if (!db || !sessionId) return;
738: 
739:   try {
740:     const countStmt = db.prepare(`
741:       SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM session_sent_memories WHERE session_id = ?
742:     `);
743:     const row = countStmt.get(sessionId) as { count: number } | undefined;
744:     const count = row?.count ?? 0;
745: 
746:     if (count <= SESSION_CONFIG.maxEntriesPerSession) return;
747: 
748:     const excess = count - SESSION_CONFIG.maxEntriesPerSession;
749:     const deleteStmt = db.prepare(`
750:       DELETE FROM session_sent_memories
751:       WHERE session_id = ? AND rowid IN (
752:         SELECT rowid FROM session_sent_memories
753:         WHERE session_id = ?
754:         ORDER BY sent_at ASC
755:         LIMIT ?
756:       )
757:     `);
758:     deleteStmt.run(sessionId, sessionId, excess);
759:   } catch (error: unknown) {
760:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
761:     console.warn(`[session-manager] enforce_entry_limit failed: ${message}`);
762:   }
763: }

770:     const cutoffIso = new Date(cutoffMs).toISOString();

773:       DELETE FROM session_sent_memories WHERE sent_at < ?
774:     `);

785: /**
786:  * Clean up stale sessions across all session-related tables.
787:  * 
788:  * Targets:
789:  *   - working_memory: entries with last_focused older than threshold
790:  *   - session_sent_memories: entries with sent_at older than threshold
791:  *   - session_state: completed/interrupted sessions older than threshold
792:  * 
793:  * Preserves:
794:  *   - session_learning records (permanent, never cleaned)
795:  *   - Active sessions (session_state with status='active')
796:  * 
797:  * @param thresholdMs - Inactivity threshold in milliseconds (default: STALE_SESSION_THRESHOLD_MS / 24h)
798:  */
799: function cleanupStaleSessions(thresholdMs: number = STALE_SESSION_THRESHOLD_MS): StaleCleanupResult {
800:   if (!db) {
801:     return { success: false, workingMemoryDeleted: 0, sentMemoriesDeleted: 0, sessionStateDeleted: 0, errors: ['Database not initialized'] };
802:   }
803: 
804:   const errors: string[] = [];
805:   let workingMemoryDeleted = 0;
806:   let sentMemoriesDeleted = 0;
807:   let sessionStateDeleted = 0;
808:   const cutoffIso = new Date(Date.now() - thresholdMs).toISOString();
809: 
810:   // 1. Clean stale working_memory entries
811:   try {
812:     const wmStmt = db.prepare('DELETE FROM working_memory WHERE last_focused < ?');
813:     const wmResult = wmStmt.run(cutoffIso);
814:     workingMemoryDeleted = wmResult.changes;
815:   } catch (err: unknown) {
816:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
817:     // Table may not exist if working-memory module was never initialized
818:     if (!msg.includes('no such table')) {
819:       errors.push(`working_memory cleanup: ${msg}`);
820:     }
821:   }
822: 
823:   // 2. Clean stale session_sent_memories entries
824:   try {
825:     const smStmt = db.prepare('DELETE FROM session_sent_memories WHERE sent_at < ?');
826:     const smResult = smStmt.run(cutoffIso);
827:     sentMemoriesDeleted = smResult.changes;
828:   } catch (err: unknown) {
829:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
830:     if (!msg.includes('no such table')) {
831:       errors.push(`session_sent_memories cleanup: ${msg}`);
832:     }
833:   }
834: 
835:   // 3. Clean completed/interrupted session_state entries (NEVER clean active sessions)
836:   try {
837:     const ssStmt = db.prepare(
838:       `DELETE FROM session_state WHERE status IN ('completed', 'interrupted') AND updated_at < ?`
839:     );
840:     const ssResult = ssStmt.run(cutoffIso);
841:     sessionStateDeleted = ssResult.changes;
842:   } catch (err: unknown) {
843:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
844:     if (!msg.includes('no such table')) {
845:       errors.push(`session_state cleanup: ${msg}`);
846:     }
847:   }
848: 
849:   const totalDeleted = workingMemoryDeleted + sentMemoriesDeleted + sessionStateDeleted;
850:   if (totalDeleted > 0) {
851:     console.error(
852:       `[session-manager] Stale session cleanup: removed ${workingMemoryDeleted} working_memory, ` +
853:       `${sentMemoriesDeleted} sent_memories, ${sessionStateDeleted} session_state entries ` +
854:       `(threshold: ${Math.round(thresholdMs / 3600000)}h)`
855:     );
856:   }
857: 
858:   if (errors.length > 0) {
859:     console.warn(`[session-manager] Stale cleanup had ${errors.length} error(s): ${errors.join('; ')}`);
860:   }
861: 
862:   return {
863:     success: errors.length === 0,
864:     workingMemoryDeleted,
865:     sentMemoriesDeleted,
866:     sessionStateDeleted,
867:     errors,
868:   };
869: }

926: function filterSearchResults(sessionId: string, results: MemoryInput[]): FilterResult {
927:   if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled || !sessionId || !Array.isArray(results)) {
928:     return {
929:       filtered: results || [],
930:       dedupStats: { enabled: false, filtered: 0, total: results?.length || 0 },
931:     };
932:   }
933: 
934:   // Reserve unsent hashes while filtering so concurrent searches cannot both inject.
935:   const shouldSendMap = shouldSendMemoriesBatch(sessionId, results, true);
936:   const seenBatchHashes = new Set<string>();
937:   const filtered = results.filter((r) => {
938:     if (r.id != null && shouldSendMap.get(r.id) === false) {
939:       return false;
940:     }
941:     try {
942:       const hash = generateMemoryHash(r);
943:       if (seenBatchHashes.has(hash)) {
944:         return false;
945:       }
946:       seenBatchHashes.add(hash);
947:     } catch {
948:       // If hash generation fails unexpectedly, preserve existing behavior.
949:       return true;
950:     }
951:     return true;
952:   });
953:   const filteredCount = results.length - filtered.length;
954: 
955:   return {
956:     filtered,
957:     dedupStats: {
958:       enabled: true,
959:       filtered: filteredCount,
960:       total: results.length,
961:       tokenSavingsEstimate: filteredCount > 0 ? `~${filteredCount * 200} tokens` : '0',
962:     },
963:   };
964: }
965: 
966: function markResultsSent(sessionId: string, results: MemoryInput[]): MarkBatchResult {
967:   if (!SESSION_CONFIG.enabled || !sessionId || !Array.isArray(results) || results.length === 0) {
968:     return { success: true, markedCount: 0 };
969:   }
970: 
971:   return markMemoriesSentBatch(sessionId, results);
972: }

986: const SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_SQL = `
987:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_state (
988:     session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
989:     status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK(status IN ('active', 'completed', 'interrupted')),
990:     spec_folder TEXT,
991:     current_task TEXT,
992:     last_action TEXT,
993:     context_summary TEXT,
994:     pending_work TEXT,
995:     state_data TEXT,
996:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
997:     updated_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
998:   );
999: `;
1000: 
1001: const SESSION_STATE_INDEX_SQL: string[] = [
1002:   'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_state_status ON session_state(status);',
1003:   'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_state_updated ON session_state(updated_at);',
1004:   'CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_state_identity_scope ON session_state(tenant_id, user_id, agent_id);',
1005: ];
1006: 
1007: const SESSION_STATE_MIGRATIONS: Array<{ column: string; sql: string }> = [
1008:   { column: 'tenant_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE session_state ADD COLUMN tenant_id TEXT;' },
1009:   { column: 'user_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE session_state ADD COLUMN user_id TEXT;' },
1010:   { column: 'agent_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE session_state ADD COLUMN agent_id TEXT;' },
1011: ];
1012: 
1013: function getTableColumns(tableName: string): Set<string> {
1014:   if (!db) {
1015:     return new Set();
1016:   }
1017: 
1018:   const rows = db.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name?: string }>;
1019:   return new Set(
1020:     rows
1021:       .map((row) => row.name)
1022:       .filter((name): name is string => typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0),
1023:   );
1024: }

1026: function ensureSessionStateSchema(): InitResult {
1027:   if (!db) return { success: false, error: 'Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.' };
1028: 
1029:   try {
1030:     db.exec(SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_SQL);
1031:     const existingColumns = getTableColumns('session_state');
1032:     for (const migration of SESSION_STATE_MIGRATIONS) {
1033:       if (!existingColumns.has(migration.column)) {
1034:         db.exec(migration.sql);
1035:       }
1036:     }
1037:     for (const indexSql of SESSION_STATE_INDEX_SQL) {
1038:       db.exec(indexSql);
1039:     }
1040:     return { success: true };
1041:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1042:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1043:     console.error(`[session-manager] Session state schema creation failed: ${message}`);
1044:     return { success: false, error: message };
1045:   }

1054:   try {
1055:     ensureSessionStateSchema();

1063:       INSERT INTO session_state (

1065:         context_summary, pending_work, state_data,

1097:     );

1183:     if (mismatch) {

1185:     }
1186:

1234:     const rows = stmt.all() as Record<string, unknown>[];
1235:     const filteredRows = rows.filter((row) => {
1236:       const storedIdentity = {
1237:         tenantId: normalizeIdentityValue(row.tenant_id),
1238:         userId: normalizeIdentityValue(row.user_id),
1239:         agentId: normalizeIdentityValue(row.agent_id),
1240:       };
1241:       return getIdentityMismatch(storedIdentity, scope) === null;
1242:     });
1243: 
1244:     return {
1245:       success: true,
1246:       sessions: filteredRows.map((row) => ({
1247:         sessionId: row.session_id as string,
1248:         specFolder: (row.spec_folder as string) || null,
1249:         currentTask: (row.current_task as string) || null,
1250:         lastAction: (row.last_action as string) || null,
1251:         contextSummary: (row.context_summary as string) || null,
1252:         pendingWork: (row.pending_work as string) || null,
1253:         updatedAt: row.updated_at as string,
1254:       })),
1255:     };
1256:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1257:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1258:     console.error(`[session-manager] get_interrupted_sessions failed: ${message}`);
1259:     return { success: false, sessions: [], error: message };
1260:   }

1265: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

1267: function generateContinueSessionMd(sessionState: ContinueSessionInput): string {
1268:   const {
1269:     sessionId,
1270:     specFolder,
1271:     currentTask,
1272:     lastAction,
1273:     contextSummary,
1274:     pendingWork,
1275:     data,
1276:   } = sessionState;

1278:   const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();

1281:     year: 'numeric',
1282:     month: 'long',

1293: 
1294:   const content = `# CONTINUE SESSION
1295:

1297: > **Purpose:** Enable seamless session recovery after context compaction, crashes, or breaks.

1299: 
1300: ---
1301: 
1302: ## Session State
1303: 
1304: | Field | Value |
1305: |-------|-------|
1306: | **Session ID** | \`${sessionId || 'N/A'}\` |
1307: | **Spec Folder** | ${specFolder || 'N/A'} |
1308: | **Current Task** | ${currentTask || 'N/A'} |
1309: | **Last Action** | ${lastAction || 'N/A'} |
1310: | **Status** | Active |
1311: | **Updated** | ${timestamp} |
1312: 
1313: ---
1314: 
1315: ## Context Summary
1316: 
1317: ${contextSummary || '_No context summary available._'}
1318: 
1319: ---
1320: 
1321: ## Pending Work
1322: 
1323: ${pendingWork || '_No pending work recorded._'}
1324: 
1325: ---
1326: 
1327: ## Quick Resume
1328: 
1329: To continue this session, use:
1330: 
1331: \`\`\`
1332: ${resumeCommand}
1333: \`\`\`
1334: 
1335: ${data ? `
1336: ---
1337: 
1338: ## Additional State Data
1339: 
1340: \`\`\`json
1341: ${JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)}
1342: \`\`\`
1343: ` : ''}
1344: ---
1345: 
1346: *This file is auto-generated on session checkpoint. It provides a human-readable recovery mechanism alongside SQLite persistence.*
1347: `;
1348: 
1349:   return content;
1350: }

1352: function writeContinueSessionMd(sessionId: string, specFolderPath: string): CheckpointResult {
1353:   if (!sessionId || !specFolderPath) {
1354:     return { success: false, error: 'sessionId and specFolderPath are required' };
1355:   }
1356: 
1357:   try {
1358:     const recoverResult = recoverState(sessionId);

1360:       const minimalState: ContinueSessionInput = {
1361:         sessionId,

1364:       const content = generateContinueSessionMd(minimalState);
1365:       const filePath = path.join(specFolderPath, 'CONTINUE_SESSION.md');

1371:     const filePath = path.join(specFolderPath, 'CONTINUE_SESSION.md');
1372:     fs.writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf8');
1373: 
1374:     return { success: true, filePath };
1375:   } catch (error: unknown) {

1397:   return { success: true, note: 'State saved to SQLite, no spec folder for CONTINUE_SESSION.md' };

408:     ? (db.prepare(
409:       `SELECT rowid, embedding
410:        FROM ${cacheTable(db)}
411:        WHERE content_hash = ?
412:          AND profile_key = ?
413:          AND input_kind = ?
414:          AND model_id = ?
415:          AND dimensions = ?`,
416:     ) as Database.Statement).get(contentHash, profileKey, inputKind, modelId, dimensions) as
417:       | { rowid: number; embedding: Buffer }
418:       | undefined
419:     : (db.prepare(
420:       `SELECT rowid, embedding
421:        FROM ${cacheTable(db)}
422:        WHERE content_hash = ?
423:          AND model_id = ?
424:          AND dimensions = ?
425:        ORDER BY
426:          CASE WHEN profile_key = '' AND input_kind = 'document' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
427:          last_used_at DESC
428:        LIMIT 1`,
429:     ) as Database.Statement).get(contentHash, modelId, dimensions) as
430:       | { rowid: number; embedding: Buffer }
431:       | undefined;
432: 
433:   if (!row) {
434:     cacheMisses += 1;
435:     return null;
436:   }
437: 
438:   cacheHits += 1;
439: 
440:   // Update last_used_at on cache hit
441:   (db.prepare(
442:     `UPDATE ${cacheTable(db)} SET last_used_at = datetime('now') WHERE rowid = ?`,
443:   ) as Database.Statement).run(row.rowid);
444: 
445:   return row.embedding;
446: }
447: 
448: /* --- 5. CACHE STORE --- */
449: 
450: /**
451:  * Store an embedding in the cache.
452:  * Uses INSERT OR REPLACE so duplicate (content_hash, model_id, dimensions)
453:  * rows are overwritten with the latest embedding, while allowing multiple
454:  * dimension variants to coexist for the same content/model pair.
455:  *
456:  * NOTE: The count-then-delete below is a non-transactional read-then-update
457:  * pattern. Under concurrent writers the cache may briefly exceed
458:  * MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES, but this is acceptable for a performance cache — the
459:  * next store call will trim the overshoot.
460:  *
461:  * @param db - better-sqlite3 database instance
462:  * @param contentHash - SHA-256 hex digest of the content
463:  * @param modelId - Embedding model identifier
464:  * @param embedding - Raw embedding buffer
465:  * @param dimensions - Number of embedding dimensions
466:  */
467: function storeEmbedding(
468:   db: Database.Database,
469:   contentHash: string,
470:   modelId: string,
471:   embedding: Buffer,
472:   dimensions: number,
473:   options: EmbeddingCacheKeyOptions = {},
474: ): void {
475:   const profileKey = normalizeProfileKey(options.profileKey);
476:   const inputKind = normalizeInputKind(options.inputKind);
477: 
478:   (db.prepare(
479:     `INSERT INTO ${cacheTable(db)}
480:        (content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, embedding, dimensions, last_used_at)
481:      VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
482:      ON CONFLICT(content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, dimensions)
483:      DO UPDATE SET
484:        embedding = excluded.embedding,
485:        last_used_at = datetime('now')`,
486:   ) as Database.Statement).run(contentHash, profileKey, inputKind, modelId, embedding, dimensions);
487: 
488:   evictIfOverBudget(db);
489: }
490: 
491: /* --- 6. LRU EVICTION --- */
492: 
493: /**
494:  * Evict rows until byte and secondary entry budgets are satisfied.
495:  *
496:  * @param db - better-sqlite3 database instance
497:  * @returns Number of evicted rows
498:  */
499: function evictIfOverBudget(db: Database.Database): number {
500:   const budgets = getCacheBudgetConfig();
501:   let deleted = 0;
502: 
503:   deleted += evictLruUntilUnderBudget(db, '1 = 1', [], budgets.maxBytes);
504: 
505:   const profileRows = (db.prepare(`
506:     SELECT profile_key, COALESCE(SUM(${BYTE_ESTIMATE_SQL}), 0) AS bytes
507:     FROM ${cacheTable(db)}
508:     GROUP BY profile_key
509:     HAVING COALESCE(SUM(${BYTE_ESTIMATE_SQL}), 0) > ?
510:   `) as Database.Statement).all(budgets.profileMaxBytes) as Array<{ profile_key: string; bytes: number }>;
511:   for (const row of profileRows) {
512:     deleted += evictLruUntilUnderBudget(
513:       db,
514:       'profile_key = ?',
515:       [row.profile_key],
516:       budgets.profileMaxBytes,
517:     );
518:   }
519: 
520:   deleted += evictLruUntilUnderBudget(
521:     db,
522:     "input_kind = 'query'",
523:     [],
524:     budgets.queryMaxBytes,
525:   );
526:   deleted += evictProfileEntryOverflow(db, budgets.maxEntriesPerProfile);
527: 
528:   if (deleted > 0) {
529:     shrinkSqliteMemory(db);
530:   }
531: 
532:   return deleted;
533: }
534: 
535: /**
536:  * Evict cache entries whose last_used_at is older than maxAgeDays.
537:  *
538:  * @param db - better-sqlite3 database instance
539:  * @param maxAgeDays - Maximum age in days before eviction
540:  * @returns Number of evicted entries
541:  */
542: function evictOldEntries(db: Database.Database, maxAgeDays: number): number {
543:   const result = (db.prepare(
544:     `DELETE FROM ${cacheTable(db)}
545:      WHERE last_used_at < datetime('now', ? || ' days')`,
546:   ) as Database.Statement).run(`-${maxAgeDays}`);
547: 
548:   return (result as { changes: number }).changes;
549: }
550: 
551: /* --- 7. STATISTICS --- */
552: 
553: /**
554:  * Return runtime hit/miss statistics for the embedding cache.
555:  *
556:  * @returns Cache hit/miss counters and derived hit rate
557:  */
558: function getCacheStats(): EmbeddingCacheHitStats;
559: 
560: /**
561:  * Return aggregate statistics about the embedding cache.
562:  *
563:  * @param db - better-sqlite3 database instance
564:  * @returns Cache statistics including total entries, size, oldest/newest timestamps
565:  */
566: function getCacheStats(db: Database.Database): EmbeddingCacheStats;
567: function getCacheStats(db?: Database.Database): EmbeddingCacheStats | EmbeddingCacheHitStats {
568:   if (!db) {
569:     const totalLookups = cacheHits + cacheMisses;
570: 
571:     return {
572:       hits: cacheHits,
573:       misses: cacheMisses,
574:       hitRate: totalLookups > 0 ? cacheHits / totalLookups : 0,
575:     };
576:   }
577: 
578:   const row = (db.prepare(`
579:     SELECT
580:       COUNT(*) AS total_entries,
581:       COALESCE(SUM(${BYTE_ESTIMATE_SQL}), 0) AS total_size_bytes,
582:       MIN(last_used_at) AS oldest_entry,
583:       MAX(last_used_at) AS newest_entry
584:     FROM ${cacheTable(db)}
585:   `) as Database.Statement).get() as {
586:     total_entries: number;
587:     total_size_bytes: number;
588:     oldest_entry: string | null;
589:     newest_entry: string | null;
590:   };
591: 
592:   return {
593:     totalEntries: row.total_entries,
594:     totalSizeBytes: row.total_size_bytes,
595:     oldestEntry: row.oldest_entry,
596:     newestEntry: row.newest_entry,
597:   };
598: }
599: 
600: function getByteEstimate(db: Database.Database): EmbeddingCacheByteEstimate {
601:   const row = (db.prepare(`
602:     SELECT
603:       COUNT(*) AS entries,
604:       COALESCE(SUM(${BYTE_ESTIMATE_SQL}), 0) AS approx_bytes
605:     FROM ${cacheTable(db)}
606:   `) as Database.Statement).get() as {
607:     entries: number;
608:     approx_bytes: number;
609:   };
610: 
611:   return {
612:     entries: row.entries,
613:     approxBytes: row.approx_bytes,
614:   };
615: }
616: 
617: function getEmbeddingCacheByProfileStats(
618:   db: Database.Database,
619: ): Record<string, EmbeddingCacheProfileStats> {
620:   const rows = (db.prepare(`
621:     SELECT
622:       profile_key,
623:       input_kind,
624:       COUNT(*) AS entries,
625:       COALESCE(SUM(${BYTE_ESTIMATE_SQL}), 0) AS bytes
626:     FROM ${cacheTable(db)}
627:     GROUP BY profile_key, input_kind
628:   `) as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{
629:     profile_key: string;
630:     input_kind: EmbeddingInputKind;
631:     entries: number;
632:     bytes: number;
633:   }>;
634: 
635:   const profiles: Record<string, EmbeddingCacheProfileStats> = {};
636:   for (const row of rows) {
637:     const profileKey = row.profile_key;
638:     profiles[profileKey] ??= {
639:       entries: 0,
640:       bytes: 0,
641:       by_kind: {
642:         document: { entries: 0, bytes: 0 },
643:         query: { entries: 0, bytes: 0 },
644:       },
645:     };
646: 
647:     const inputKind = normalizeInputKind(row.input_kind);
648:     profiles[profileKey].entries += row.entries;
649:     profiles[profileKey].bytes += row.bytes;
650:     profiles[profileKey].by_kind[inputKind] = {
651:       entries: row.entries,
652:       bytes: row.bytes,
653:     };
654:   }
655: 
656:   return profiles;
657: }
658: 
659: /* --- 8. CLEAR --- */
660: 
661: /**
662:  * Remove all entries from the embedding cache.
663:  *
664:  * @param db - better-sqlite3 database instance
665:  */
666: function clearCache(db: Database.Database): void {
667:   db.exec(`DELETE FROM ${cacheTable(db)}`);
668: }
669: 
670: /**
671:  * Remove all cached embedding variants for a content hash.
672:  *
673:  * Retention sweeps use this to delete derived semantic cache data alongside
674:  * the source memory row. The cache is content-addressed rather than row-bound,
675:  * so this intentionally removes all model/dimension variants for the content.
676:  *
677:  * @param db - better-sqlite3 database instance
678:  * @param contentHash - SHA-256 hex digest of the deleted content
679:  * @returns Number of deleted cache rows
680:  */
681: function deleteByContentHash(db: Database.Database, contentHash: string): number {
682:   const result = (db.prepare(
683:     `DELETE FROM ${cacheTable(db)} WHERE content_hash = ?`,
684:   ) as Database.Statement).run(contentHash);
685: 
686:   return (result as { changes: number }).changes;
687: }
688: 
689: /* --- 9. CONTENT HASHING --- */
690: 
691: /**
692:  * Compute a SHA-256 hex digest of the given content string.
693:  * Matches the pattern used elsewhere in the codebase
694:  * (e.g. memory-parser.ts computeContentHash).
695:  *
696:  * @param content - Raw content string to hash
697:  * @returns SHA-256 hex digest
698:  */
699: function computeContentHash(content: string): string {
700:   return createHash('sha256').update(content, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
701: }
702: 
703: function getActiveEmbeddingProfileKey(
704:   db: Database.Database,
705:   fallbackModelId = '',
706:   fallbackDimensions = 0,
707: ): string {
708:   const metadataProfileKey = readActiveProfileKeyFromMetadata(db);
709:   if (metadataProfileKey) {
710:     return metadataProfileKey;
711:   }
712: 
713:   const normalizedModelId = fallbackModelId.trim();
714:   return normalizedModelId && fallbackDimensions > 0
715:     ? `${normalizedModelId}:${fallbackDimensions}`
716:     : '';
717: }
718: 
719: /* --- 10. EXPORTS --- */
720: 
721: export {
722:   initEmbeddingCache,
723:   lookupEmbedding,
724:   storeEmbedding,
725:   evictIfOverBudget,
726:   evictOldEntries,
727:   getCacheStats,
728:   getByteEstimate,
729:   getEmbeddingCacheByProfileStats,
730:   clearCache,
731:   deleteByContentHash,
732:   computeContentHash,
733:   getActiveEmbeddingProfileKey,
734: };
735: 
736: /**
737:  * Re-exports related public types.
738:  */
739: export type {
740:   EmbeddingCacheEntry,
741:   EmbeddingInputKind,
742:   EmbeddingCacheKeyOptions,
743:   EmbeddingCacheStats,
744:   EmbeddingCacheHitStats,
745:   EmbeddingCacheByteEstimate,
746:   EmbeddingCacheKindStats,
747:   EmbeddingCacheProfileStats,
748: };

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Eval Db
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Evaluation database and schema
5: // Separate evaluation database with 5 tables for tracking
6: // Retrieval quality metrics, ground truth, and eval results.
7: // Node stdlib
8: import * as path from 'path';
9: import * as fs from 'fs';
10: 
11: // External packages
12: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
13: 
14: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
15:    1. CONFIGURATION
16: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
17: 
18: // Follow same env-var precedence as main DB (vector-index-impl.ts)
19: // SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR (canonical) > MEMORY_DB_DIR (legacy) > default
20: const DEFAULT_DB_DIR: string =
21:   process.env.SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR ||
22:   process.env.MEMORY_DB_DIR ||
23:   path.resolve(import.meta.dirname, '../../database');
24: 
25: const EVAL_DB_FILENAME = 'speckit-eval.db';
26: 
27: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28:    2. MODULE STATE (singleton pattern matching main DB)
29: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
30: 
31: let evalDb: Database.Database | null = null;
32: let evalDbPath: string | null = null;
33: 
34: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35:    3. SCHEMA DDL
36: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
37: 
38: const EVAL_SCHEMA_SQL = `
39:   -- Evaluation queries with metadata
40:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_queries (
41:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
42:     query TEXT NOT NULL,
43:     intent TEXT,
44:     spec_folder TEXT,
45:     expected_memory_ids TEXT,
46:     difficulty TEXT DEFAULT 'medium',
47:     category TEXT,
48:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
49:     updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
50:   );
51: 
52:   -- Per-channel results for each query evaluation
53:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_channel_results (
54:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
55:     eval_run_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
56:     query_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
57:     channel TEXT NOT NULL,
58:     result_memory_ids TEXT,
59:     scores TEXT,
60:     latency_ms REAL,
61:     hit_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
62:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
63:   );
64: 
65:   -- Final fused results after RRF/fusion
66:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_final_results (
67:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
68:     eval_run_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
69:     query_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
70:     result_memory_ids TEXT,
71:     scores TEXT,
72:     fusion_method TEXT DEFAULT 'rrf',
73:     latency_ms REAL,
74:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
75:   );
76: 
77:   -- Ground truth relevance judgments
78:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_ground_truth (
79:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
80:     query_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
81:     memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
82:     relevance INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
83:     annotator TEXT DEFAULT 'auto',
84:     notes TEXT,
85:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
86:     UNIQUE(query_id, memory_id)
87:   );
88: 
89:   -- Metric snapshots for tracking over time
90:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_metric_snapshots (
91:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
92:     eval_run_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
93:     metric_name TEXT NOT NULL,
94:     metric_value REAL NOT NULL,
95:     channel TEXT,
96:     query_count INTEGER,
97:     metadata TEXT,
98:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
99:   );
100: 
101:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_metric_snapshots_run
102:     ON eval_metric_snapshots (eval_run_id, metric_name, created_at);
103: 
104:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_channel_results_run
105:     ON eval_channel_results (eval_run_id, channel, query_id);
106: 
107:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_final_results_run
108:     ON eval_final_results (eval_run_id, query_id);
109: `;
110: 
111: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
112:    4. INITIALIZATION
113: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
114: 
115: /**
116:  * Initialize the evaluation database.
117:  * Creates the DB file in dataDir (same directory as main DB) and
118:  * ensures all 5 eval tables exist. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent).
119:  *
120:  * @param dataDir - Directory where the DB file will be created.
121:  *                  Defaults to same dir as main DB.
122:  */
123: function initEvalDb(dataDir?: string): Database.Database {
124:   const resolvedDir = dataDir || DEFAULT_DB_DIR;
125:   const dbPath = path.join(resolvedDir, EVAL_DB_FILENAME);
126: 
127:   // Return singleton if already initialized to the same path
128:   if (evalDb && evalDbPath === dbPath) {
129:     return evalDb;
130:   }
131: 
132:   // Close previous handle if path changed
133:   if (evalDb) {
134:     try { evalDb.close(); } catch (err) { console.warn('[eval-db] initEvalDb close-before-switch warning:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); }
135:     evalDb = null;
136:     evalDbPath = null;
137:   }
138: 
139:   // Ensure the directory exists
140:   if (!fs.existsSync(resolvedDir)) {
141:     fs.mkdirSync(resolvedDir, { recursive: true });
142:   }
143: 
144:   const db = new Database(dbPath);
145: 
146:   // Enable WAL mode for better concurrent read performance
147:   db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
148:   db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000');
149:   db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
150: 
151:   // Create all 5 eval tables (idempotent via IF NOT EXISTS)
152:   db.exec(EVAL_SCHEMA_SQL);
153: 
154:   evalDb = db;
155:   evalDbPath = dbPath;
156: 
157:   return db;
158: }
159: 
160: /**
161:  * Get the singleton eval DB instance.
162:  * Throws if initEvalDb() has not been called.
163:  */
164: function getEvalDb(): Database.Database {
165:   if (!evalDb) {
166:     throw new Error('[eval-db] Eval database not initialized. Call initEvalDb() first.');
167:   }
168:   return evalDb;
169: }
170: 
171: /**
172:  * Get the path to the eval DB file.
173:  * Returns null if not yet initialized.
174:  */
175: function getEvalDbPath(): string | null {
176:   return evalDbPath;
177: }
178: 
179: /**
180:  * Close and reset the eval DB singleton (useful for tests).
181:  */
182: function closeEvalDb(): void {
183:   if (evalDb) {
184:     try {
185:       evalDb.close();
186:     } catch (err) {
187:       console.warn('[eval-db] closeEvalDb warning:', err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
188:     }
189:     evalDb = null;
190:     evalDbPath = null;
191:   }
192: }
193: 
194: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
195:    5. EXPORTS
196: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
197: 
198: export {
199:   initEvalDb,
200:   getEvalDb,
201:   getEvalDbPath,
202:   closeEvalDb,
203:   DEFAULT_DB_DIR,
204:   EVAL_DB_FILENAME,
205: };

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Entity Extractor
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Auto entity extraction
5: // Feature-flagged via SPECKIT_AUTO_ENTITIES
6: // Pure-TS rule-based extraction, zero npm dependencies.
7: import { isEntityDenied } from './entity-denylist.js';
8: import { normalizeEntityName, computeEdgeDensity } from '../search/entity-linker.js';
9: 
10: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
11: 
12: // Re-export canonical versions from entity-linker for backward compatibility
13: export { normalizeEntityName, computeEdgeDensity };
14: 
15: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: // 1. TYPES
17: 
18: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19: /** A single entity extracted from memory content. */
20: export interface ExtractedEntity {
21:   /** The raw entity text as found in content. */
22:   text: string;
23:   /** Classification of how the entity was detected. */
24:   type: 'proper_noun' | 'technology' | 'key_phrase' | 'heading' | 'quoted';
25:   /** Number of occurrences in the source content. */
26:   frequency: number;
27: }
28: 
29: export interface RebuildAutoEntitiesOptions {
30:   specFolder?: string | null;
31:   dryRun?: boolean;
32: }
33: 
34: export interface RebuildAutoEntitiesResult {
35:   dryRun: boolean;
36:   specFolder: string | null;
37:   memoriesScanned: number;
38:   memoriesReprocessed: number;
39:   autoRowsRemoved: number;
40:   extractedEntities: number;
41:   storedEntities: number;
42:   catalogEntriesRebuilt: number;
43: }
44: 
45: interface AutoEntitySourceRow {
46:   id: number;
47:   spec_folder: string | null;
48:   content_text: string | null;
49: }
50: 
51: interface CatalogEntityRow {
52:   memory_id: number;
53:   entity_text: string;
54:   entity_type: ExtractedEntity['type'];
55: }
56: 
57: export interface RefreshAutoEntitiesForMemoryResult {
58:   removed: number;
59:   stored: number;
60:   catalogRebuilt: boolean;
61: }
62: 
63: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64: // 2. EXTRACTION RULES
65: 
66: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67: /**
68:  * Main extraction function — pure-TS rule-based, no npm deps.
69:  *
70:  * Rules applied in order:
71:  *   1. Capitalized multi-word sequences (2+ words starting with uppercase) → proper_noun
72:  *   2. Technology names from code fence annotations → technology
73:  *   3. Words after key phrases ("using", "with", "via", "implements") → key_phrase
74:  *   4. Markdown heading content (## through ####) → heading
75:  *   5. Quoted strings (double quotes, 2-50 chars) → quoted
76:  *
77:  * Results are deduplicated by normalized text with summed frequencies.
78:  *
79:  * @param content - The raw text content to extract entities from.
80:  * @returns Array of extracted entities, deduplicated and frequency-counted.
81:  */
82: export function extractEntities(content: string): ExtractedEntity[] {
83:   const raw: Array<{ text: string; type: ExtractedEntity['type'] }> = [];
84: 
85:   // Rule 1: Capitalized multi-word sequences (proper nouns)
86:   const properNounRe = /\b([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)\b/g;
87:   let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
88:   while ((match = properNounRe.exec(content)) !== null) {
89:     raw.push({ text: match[1], type: 'proper_noun' });
90:   }
91: 
92:   // Rule 2: Technology names from code fence annotations
93:   const codeFenceRe = /```(\w+)/g;
94:   while ((match = codeFenceRe.exec(content)) !== null) {
95:     raw.push({ text: match[1], type: 'technology' });
96:   }
97: 
98:   // Rule 3: Words after key phrases — keywords are case-insensitive via explicit
99:   // Alternation (no `i` flag, since continuation words must require uppercase start
100:   // To avoid capturing common English words like "and", "the"). Tokens may include
101:   // Internal dots (for names like "Node.js"), but a trailing sentence period ends
102:   // The match so we do not absorb the next sentence.
103:   const keyPhraseRe = /\b(?:[Uu]sing|[Ww]ith|[Vv]ia|[Ii]mplements)\s+([A-Za-z][\w-]*(?:\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+)*(?:\s+[A-Z][\w-]*(?:\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+)*)*)/g;
104:   while ((match = keyPhraseRe.exec(content)) !== null) {
105:     raw.push({ text: match[1], type: 'key_phrase' });
106:   }
107: 
108:   // Rule 4: Markdown heading content (## through ####)
109:   const headingRe = /^#{2,4}\s+(.+)$/gm;
110:   while ((match = headingRe.exec(content)) !== null) {
111:     raw.push({ text: match[1].trim(), type: 'heading' });
112:   }
113: 
114:   // Rule 5: Quoted strings (double quotes, 2-50 chars)
115:   const quotedRe = /"([^"]{2,50})"/g;
116:   while ((match = quotedRe.exec(content)) !== null) {
117:     raw.push({ text: match[1], type: 'quoted' });
118:   }
119: 
120:   // Deduplicate by normalized text (lowercase, trimmed), summing frequencies
121:   return deduplicateEntities(raw);
122: }
123: 
124: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125: // 3. FILTERING
126: 
127: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
128: /**
129:  * Filter entities through denylist + length checks.
130:  *
131:  * Removes:
132:  *   - Single-character entities
133:  *   - Entities where ALL words are on the denylist
134:  *   - Entities longer than 100 characters
135:  *
136:  * @param entities - Raw extracted entities to filter.
137:  * @returns Filtered array with noise removed.
138:  */
139: export function filterEntities(entities: ExtractedEntity[]): ExtractedEntity[] {
140:   return entities.filter((entity) => {
141:     // Remove single-character entities
142:     if (entity.text.length <= 1) return false;
143: 
144:     // Remove entities longer than 100 characters
145:     if (entity.text.length > 100) return false;
146: 
147:     // Remove entities where ALL words are on the denylist
148:     const words = entity.text.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean);
149:     if (words.length > 0 && words.every((w) => isEntityDenied(w))) return false;
150: 
151:     return true;
152:   });
153: }
154: 
155: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
156: // 4. STORAGE
157: 
158: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
159: /**
160:  * Store extracted entities in the memory_entities table.
161:  *
162:  * Uses INSERT OR REPLACE on the UNIQUE(memory_id, entity_text) constraint.
163:  *
164:  * @param db - An initialized better-sqlite3 Database instance.
165:  * @param memoryId - The memory_index row ID to associate entities with.
166:  * @param entities - Filtered entities to store.
167:  * @returns Count of entities stored.
168:  */
169: export function storeEntities(
170:   db: Database.Database,
171:   memoryId: number,
172:   entities: ExtractedEntity[],
173: ): { stored: number } {
174:   try {
175:     const stmt = db.prepare(`
176:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO memory_entities
177:         (memory_id, entity_text, entity_type, frequency, created_by)
178:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'auto')
179:     `);
180: 
181:     let stored = 0;
182:     const runInTransaction = db.transaction(() => {
183:       for (const entity of entities) {
184:         stmt.run(memoryId, entity.text, entity.type, entity.frequency);
185:         stored++;
186:       }
187:     });
188:     runInTransaction();
189: 
190:     return { stored };
191:   } catch (error: unknown) {
192:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
193:     console.warn(`[entity-extractor] storeEntities failed: ${msg}`);
194:     return { stored: 0 };
195:   }
196: }
197: 
198: export function refreshAutoEntitiesForMemory(
199:   db: Database.Database,
200:   memoryId: number,
201:   entities: ExtractedEntity[],
202: ): RefreshAutoEntitiesForMemoryResult {
203:   try {
204:     const deleteStmt = db.prepare(`
205:       DELETE FROM memory_entities
206:       WHERE memory_id = ?
207:         AND created_by = 'auto'
208:     `);
209:     const insertStmt = db.prepare(`
210:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO memory_entities
211:         (memory_id, entity_text, entity_type, frequency, created_by)
212:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'auto')
213:     `);
214: 
215:     let removed = 0;
216:     let stored = 0;
217:     const runInTransaction = db.transaction(() => {
218:       removed = deleteStmt.run(memoryId).changes;
219:       for (const entity of entities) {
220:         insertStmt.run(memoryId, entity.text, entity.type, entity.frequency);
221:         stored++;
222:       }
223:     });
224:     runInTransaction();
225: 
226:     if (removed > 0) {
227:       rebuildEntityCatalog(db);
228:       return { removed, stored, catalogRebuilt: true };
229:     }
230: 
231:     if (entities.length > 0) {
232:       updateEntityCatalog(db, entities);
233:     }
234: 
235:     return { removed, stored, catalogRebuilt: false };
236:   } catch (error: unknown) {
237:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
238:     console.warn(`[entity-extractor] refreshAutoEntitiesForMemory failed: ${msg}`);
239:     return { removed: 0, stored: 0, catalogRebuilt: false };
240:   }
241: }
242: 
243: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
244: // 5. ENTITY CATALOG
245: 
246: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
247: /**
248:  * Upsert entities into entity_catalog with alias normalization.
249:  *
250:  * For each entity:
251:  *   1. normalizeEntityName(text) -> canonical_name
252:  *   2. INSERT new catalog entry or UPDATE existing:
253:  *      - Increment memory_count
254:  *      - Append text as alias if not already present
255:  *
256:  * @param db - An initialized better-sqlite3 Database instance.
257:  * @param entities - Filtered entities to catalog.
258:  * @returns Count of entities upserted.
259:  */
260: export function updateEntityCatalog(
261:   db: Database.Database,
262:   entities: ExtractedEntity[],
263: ): { upserted: number } {
264:   try {
265:     let upserted = 0;
266: 
267:     const selectStmt = db.prepare(
268:       'SELECT id, aliases, memory_count FROM entity_catalog WHERE canonical_name = ?',
269:     );
270:     const insertStmt = db.prepare(`
271:       INSERT INTO entity_catalog (canonical_name, aliases, entity_type, memory_count, created_at)
272:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, 1, datetime('now'))
273:     `);
274:     const updateStmt = db.prepare(`
275:       UPDATE entity_catalog
276:       SET aliases = ?, memory_count = memory_count + 1
277:       WHERE id = ?
278:     `);
279: 
280:     const runInTransaction = db.transaction(() => {
281:       for (const entity of entities) {
282:         const canonical = normalizeEntityName(entity.text);
283:         if (!canonical) continue;
284: 
285:         const existing = selectStmt.get(canonical) as
286:           | { id: number; aliases: string; memory_count: number }
287:           | undefined;
288: 
289:         if (existing) {
290:           // Append alias if new variant
291:           let aliases: string[];
292:           try {
293:             aliases = JSON.parse(existing.aliases);
294:             if (!Array.isArray(aliases)) aliases = [];
295:           } catch {
296:             aliases = [];
297:           }
298: 
299:           if (!aliases.includes(entity.text)) {
300:             aliases.push(entity.text);
301:           }
302: 
303:           updateStmt.run(JSON.stringify(aliases), existing.id);
304:         } else {
305:           // Insert new catalog entry
306:           const aliases = JSON.stringify([entity.text]);
307:           insertStmt.run(canonical, aliases, entity.type);
308:         }
309: 
310:         upserted++;
311:       }
312:     });
313:     runInTransaction();
314: 
315:     return { upserted };
316:   } catch (error: unknown) {
317:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
318:     console.warn(`[entity-extractor] updateEntityCatalog failed: ${msg}`);
319:     return { upserted: 0 };
320:   }
321: }
322: 
323: /**
324:  * Rebuild entity_catalog deterministically from current memory_entities rows.
325:  */
326: export function rebuildEntityCatalog(db: Database.Database): { rebuilt: number } {
327:   try {
328:     const rows = db.prepare(`
329:       SELECT memory_id, entity_text, entity_type
330:       FROM memory_entities
331:       ORDER BY id ASC
332:     `).all() as CatalogEntityRow[];
333: 
334:     const aggregates = new Map<string, {
335:       aliases: Set<string>;
336:       entityType: ExtractedEntity['type'];
337:       memoryIds: Set<number>;
338:     }>();
339: 
340:     for (const row of rows) {
341:       const canonical = normalizeEntityName(row.entity_text);
342:       if (!canonical) {
343:         continue;
344:       }
345: 
346:       const existing = aggregates.get(canonical);
347:       if (existing) {
348:         existing.aliases.add(row.entity_text);
349:         existing.memoryIds.add(row.memory_id);
350:         continue;
351:       }
352: 
353:       aggregates.set(canonical, {
354:         aliases: new Set([row.entity_text]),
355:         entityType: row.entity_type,
356:         memoryIds: new Set([row.memory_id]),
357:       });
358:     }
359: 
360:     const deleteStmt = db.prepare('DELETE FROM entity_catalog');
361:     const insertStmt = db.prepare(`
362:       INSERT INTO entity_catalog (canonical_name, aliases, entity_type, memory_count, created_at)
363:       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
364:     `);
365: 
366:     const runInTransaction = db.transaction(() => {
367:       deleteStmt.run();
368: 
369:       for (const canonical of Array.from(aggregates.keys()).sort()) {
370:         const aggregate = aggregates.get(canonical);
371:         if (!aggregate) {
372:           continue;
373:         }
374: 
375:         insertStmt.run(
376:           canonical,
377:           JSON.stringify(Array.from(aggregate.aliases).sort()),
378:           aggregate.entityType,
379:           aggregate.memoryIds.size,
380:         );
381:       }
382:     });
383:     runInTransaction();
384: 
385:     return { rebuilt: aggregates.size };
386:   } catch (error: unknown) {
387:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
388:     console.warn(`[entity-extractor] rebuildEntityCatalog failed: ${msg}`);
389:     return { rebuilt: 0 };
390:   }
391: }
392: 
393: /**
394:  * Rebuild auto-generated entity rows from current memory content.
395:  *
396:  * This provides a deterministic cleanup path for pre-fix entity rows by
397:  * deleting only `created_by='auto'` entries in scope, re-extracting from the
398:  * live `memory_index.content_text`, and then rebuilding `entity_catalog`
399:  * exactly from the resulting entity rows.
400:  */
401: export function rebuildAutoEntities(
402:   db: Database.Database,
403:   options: RebuildAutoEntitiesOptions = {},
404: ): RebuildAutoEntitiesResult {
405:   const specFolder = typeof options.specFolder === 'string' && options.specFolder.trim().length > 0
406:     ? options.specFolder.trim()
407:     : null;
408:   const dryRun = options.dryRun === true;
409: 
410:   const memories = db.prepare(`
411:     SELECT id, spec_folder, content_text
412:     FROM memory_index
413:     WHERE (? IS NULL OR spec_folder = ?)
414:     ORDER BY id ASC
415:   `).all(specFolder, specFolder) as AutoEntitySourceRow[];
416: 
417:   const extractedByMemory = memories.map((memory) => {
418:     const filtered = filterEntities(extractEntities(memory.content_text ?? ''));
419:     return { memoryId: memory.id, entities: filtered, hasContent: (memory.content_text ?? '').trim().length > 0 };
420:   });
421: 
422:   const autoRowCount = db.prepare(`
423:     SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
424:     FROM memory_entities me
425:     WHERE me.created_by = 'auto'
426:       AND EXISTS (
427:         SELECT 1
428:         FROM memory_index m
429:         WHERE m.id = me.memory_id
430:           AND (? IS NULL OR m.spec_folder = ?)
431:       )
432:   `).get(specFolder, specFolder) as { count?: number };
433: 
434:   const extractedEntities = extractedByMemory.reduce((sum, row) => sum + row.entities.length, 0);
435:   const memoriesReprocessed = extractedByMemory.filter((row) => row.hasContent).length;
436: 
437:   if (dryRun) {
438:     return {
439:       dryRun: true,
440:       specFolder,
441:       memoriesScanned: memories.length,
442:       memoriesReprocessed,
443:       autoRowsRemoved: autoRowCount.count ?? 0,
444:       extractedEntities,
445:       storedEntities: extractedEntities,
446:       catalogEntriesRebuilt: 0,
447:     };
448:   }
449: 
450:   const deleteAutoRows = db.prepare(`
451:     DELETE FROM memory_entities
452:     WHERE created_by = 'auto'
453:       AND EXISTS (
454:         SELECT 1
455:         FROM memory_index m
456:         WHERE m.id = memory_entities.memory_id
457:           AND (? IS NULL OR m.spec_folder = ?)
458:       )
459:   `);
460: 
461:   let storedEntities = 0;
462:   const runInTransaction = db.transaction(() => {
463:     deleteAutoRows.run(specFolder, specFolder);
464: 
465:     for (const row of extractedByMemory) {
466:       if (row.entities.length === 0) {
467:         continue;
468:       }
469: 
470:       storedEntities += storeEntities(db, row.memoryId, row.entities).stored;
471:     }
472:   });
473:   runInTransaction();
474: 
475:   const rebuiltCatalog = rebuildEntityCatalog(db);
476: 
477:   return {
478:     dryRun: false,
479:     specFolder,
480:     memoriesScanned: memories.length,
481:     memoriesReprocessed,
482:     autoRowsRemoved: autoRowCount.count ?? 0,
483:     extractedEntities,
484:     storedEntities,
485:     catalogEntriesRebuilt: rebuiltCatalog.rebuilt,
486:   };
487: }
488: 
489: // 6. INTERNAL HELPERS (exported for testing)
490: /**
491:  * Deduplicate raw extraction results by normalized text.
492:  * Entries with the same normalized form are merged, summing frequencies.
493:  * The first occurrence's type wins.
494:  */
495: function deduplicateEntities(
496:   raw: Array<{ text: string; type: ExtractedEntity['type'] }>,
497: ): ExtractedEntity[] {
498:   const map = new Map<string, ExtractedEntity>();
499: 
500:   for (const item of raw) {
501:     const key = item.text.toLowerCase().trim();
502:     const existing = map.get(key);
503:     if (existing) {
504:       existing.frequency += 1;
505:     } else {
506:       map.set(key, { text: item.text, type: item.type, frequency: 1 });
507:     }
508:   }
509: 
510:   return Array.from(map.values());
511: }
512: 
513: /**
514:  * Internal helpers exported for testing via __testables.
515:  */
516: export const __testables = {
517:   deduplicateEntities,
518:   normalizeEntityName,
519: };

263:   options: SanitizeEmbeddingFailureOptions = {},
264: ): string | null {
265:   const failure = sanitizeEmbeddingFailure(raw, options);
266:   return failure.publicMessage.length > 0 ? failure.publicMessage : null;

269: export function sanitizeAndLogEmbeddingFailure(
270:   context: string,
271:   raw: unknown,
272:   options: SanitizeEmbeddingFailureOptions = {},
273: ): string | null {
274:   const failure = sanitizeEmbeddingFailure(raw, options);
275:   if (failure.publicMessage.length === 0) {
276:     return null;
277:   }
278: 
279:   if (failure.sanitized) {
280:     console.error(context, {
281:       sanitized: {
282:         code: failure.code,
283:         provider: failure.provider,
284:         errorType: failure.errorType,
285:       },
286:       redacted: true,
287:     });

289: 
290:   return failure.publicMessage;

293: function claimRetryCandidate(memory: RetryMemoryRow): RetryClaimResult {
294:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
295:   const previousStatus = getRetryStatus(memory);
296:   if (!db || previousStatus === null) {
297:     return { claimed: false, previousStatus };
298:   }
299: 
300:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
301:   const retryCount = getRetryCountValue(memory);
302:   const lastRetryAt = getLastRetryAtValue(memory);
303:   // Concurrency: atomic claim prevents double-processing of retry jobs
304:   const result = previousStatus === 'pending'
305:     ? db.prepare(`
306:         UPDATE memory_index
307:         SET embedding_status = 'retry',
308:             last_retry_at = ?,
309:             updated_at = ?
310:         WHERE id = ?
311:           AND embedding_status = 'pending'
312:           AND retry_count = ?
313:           AND ((? IS NULL AND last_retry_at IS NULL) OR last_retry_at = ?)
314:       `).run(now, now, memory.id, retryCount, lastRetryAt, lastRetryAt)
315:     : db.prepare(`
316:         UPDATE memory_index
317:         SET last_retry_at = ?,
318:             updated_at = ?
319:         WHERE id = ?
320:           AND embedding_status = 'retry'
321:           AND retry_count = ?
322:           AND ((? IS NULL AND last_retry_at IS NULL) OR last_retry_at = ?)
323:       `).run(now, now, memory.id, retryCount, lastRetryAt, lastRetryAt);
324: 
325:   return {
326:     claimed: result.changes > 0,
327:     previousStatus,
328:   };
329: }

331: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
332:    2. CONFIGURATION
333: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
334: 
335: // Backoff delays in milliseconds (1min, 5min, 15min)
336: const BACKOFF_DELAYS: number[] = [
337:   60 * 1000,
338:   5 * 60 * 1000,
339:   15 * 60 * 1000,
340: ];
341: 
342: const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
343: const MAX_RETRY_QUEUE_PENDING = parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_PENDING', 1_000);
344: const MAX_RETRY_QUEUE_AGE_MS = parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_AGE_MS', 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
345: 
346: // 005-003: Read retry-retention limits at call-time so tuned env (SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_*)
347: // applies to a long-lived daemon without depending on the exact restart sequence. The
348: // module-load constants above remain the boot-time snapshot exported for diagnostics/tests.
349: function getMaxRetryQueuePending(): number {
350:   return parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_PENDING', 1_000);
351: }
352: 
353: function getMaxRetryQueueAgeMs(): number {
354:   return parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_RETRY_QUEUE_MAX_AGE_MS', 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
355: }
356: 
357: // Background retry job configuration
358: // Env-overrideable
359: //   SPECKIT_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS — interval between background batches (default 300000 = 5 min)
360: //   SPECKIT_RETRY_BATCH_SIZE  — items processed per batch (default 5)
361: //   SPECKIT_RETRY_ENABLED     — set to "false" to disable the background loop entirely (default enabled)
362: const BACKGROUND_JOB_CONFIG: BackgroundJobConfig = {
363:   intervalMs: parseInt(process.env.SPECKIT_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS || String(5 * 60 * 1000), 10),
364:   batchSize: parseInt(process.env.SPECKIT_RETRY_BATCH_SIZE || '5', 10),
365:   enabled: (process.env.SPECKIT_RETRY_ENABLED ?? 'true') !== 'false',
366: };
367: 
368: // Background job state
369: let backgroundJobInterval: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
370: let backgroundJobRunning = false;
371: let shutdownRequested = false;
372: let retryAbortController: AbortController | null = null;
373: 
374: // In-memory retry telemetry (never persisted to DB — read by getEmbeddingRetryStats)
375: let lastBackgroundRunAt: string | null = null;
376: let totalRetryAttempts = 0;
377: const retryHealthSnapshot: EmbeddingRetryStats = {
378:   pending: 0,
379:   failed: 0,
380:   retryAttempts: 0,
381:   circuitBreakerOpen: false,
382:   lastRun: null,
383:   queueDepth: 0,
384: };
385: 
386: // circuit breaker — prevents the background retry job from
387: // Hammering the embedding API when the provider is entirely down. After
388: // PROVIDER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD consecutive failures across any items, the
389: // Circuit opens for PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS, causing retryEmbedding to skip
390: // The API call and return a transient error instead.
391: const PROVIDER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 5;
392: const PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS = 120_000; // 2 minutes
393: const CIRCUIT_TRANSITION_WINDOW_MS = 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 minutes
394: const FLAPPING_THRESHOLD = 2;
395: const circuitTransitions: number[] = [];
396: 
397: let providerFailures = 0;
398: let providerCircuitOpenedAt: number | null = null;
399: 
400: function parsePositiveIntEnv(name: string, fallback: number): number {
401:   const raw = process.env[name];
402:   if (!raw) return fallback;
403: 
404:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
405:   return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : fallback;
406: }
407: 
408: function recordCircuitTransition(): void {
409:   const now = Date.now();
410:   circuitTransitions.push(now);
411:   while (circuitTransitions.length > 0 && circuitTransitions[0] < now - CIRCUIT_TRANSITION_WINDOW_MS) {
412:     circuitTransitions.shift();
413:   }
414: }
415: 
416: function getCircuitFlapState(): { flapping: boolean; transitionsInLast10Min: number } {
417:   const now = Date.now();
418:   const recent = circuitTransitions.filter(t => t >= now - CIRCUIT_TRANSITION_WINDOW_MS);
419:   return { flapping: recent.length > FLAPPING_THRESHOLD, transitionsInLast10Min: recent.length };
420: }
421: 
422: function isProviderCircuitOpen(): boolean {
423:   if (providerCircuitOpenedAt === null) return false;
424:   if (Date.now() - providerCircuitOpenedAt >= PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS) {
425:     providerCircuitOpenedAt = null;
426:     providerFailures = 0;
427:     recordCircuitTransition();
428:     return false;
429:   }
430:   return true;
431: }
432: 
433: function recordProviderSuccess(): void {
434:   providerFailures = 0;
435:   providerCircuitOpenedAt = null;
436:   retryHealthSnapshot.circuitBreakerOpen = false;
437: }
438: 
439: function recordProviderFailure(): void {
440:   providerFailures++;
441:   if (providerFailures >= PROVIDER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD && providerCircuitOpenedAt === null) {
442:     providerCircuitOpenedAt = Date.now();
443:     recordCircuitTransition();
444:     console.warn(`[retry-manager] Embedding provider circuit breaker OPEN after ${providerFailures} consecutive failures. Cooldown: ${PROVIDER_COOLDOWN_MS}ms`);
445:   }
446:   retryHealthSnapshot.circuitBreakerOpen = isProviderCircuitOpen();
447: }
448: 
449: function applyRetryHealthTransition(previousStatus: string | null | undefined, nextStatus: string): void {
450:   if (previousStatus === 'pending') {
451:     retryHealthSnapshot.pending = Math.max(0, retryHealthSnapshot.pending - 1);
452:   }
453: 
454:   if (previousStatus === 'pending' || previousStatus === 'retry') {
455:     retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth = Math.max(0, retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth - 1);
456:   }
457: 
458:   if (nextStatus === 'pending') {
459:     retryHealthSnapshot.pending++;
460:     retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth++;
461:   }
462: 
463:   if (nextStatus === 'retry') {
464:     retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth++;
465:   }
466: 
467:   if (previousStatus === 'failed') {
468:     retryHealthSnapshot.failed = Math.max(0, retryHealthSnapshot.failed - 1);
469:   }
470: 
471:   if (nextStatus === 'failed') {
472:     retryHealthSnapshot.failed++;
473:   }
474: }
475: 
476: function refreshRetryHealthSnapshot(stats: RetryStats): void {
477:   retryHealthSnapshot.pending = stats.pending;
478:   retryHealthSnapshot.failed = stats.failed;
479:   retryHealthSnapshot.retryAttempts = totalRetryAttempts;
480:   retryHealthSnapshot.circuitBreakerOpen = isProviderCircuitOpen();
481:   retryHealthSnapshot.lastRun = lastBackgroundRunAt;
482:   retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth = stats.queue_size;
483: }
484: 
485: function enforceRetryRetentionLimits(
486:   maxPending = getMaxRetryQueuePending(),
487:   maxAgeMs = getMaxRetryQueueAgeMs(),
488:   now: Date = new Date(),
489: ): { expired: number; overflow: number } {
490:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
491:   if (!db) return { expired: 0, overflow: 0 };
492: 
493:   const nowIso = now.toISOString();
494:   const oldestAllowedIso = new Date(now.getTime() - maxAgeMs).toISOString();
495:   // Retention is non-destructive for never-attempted clean `pending` rows.
496:   // Only rows that have actually been attempted (status='retry' OR retry_count>0) are
497:   // subject to max-age expiry and pending-cap overflow; clean `pending` work waits to be
498:   // embedded instead of being parked as `failed` before the drain can claim it.
499:   const expired = db.prepare(`
500:     UPDATE memory_index
501:     SET embedding_status = 'failed',
502:         failure_reason = 'Retry retention max age exceeded',
503:         updated_at = ?
504:     WHERE embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
505:       AND (embedding_status = 'retry' OR COALESCE(retry_count, 0) > 0)
506:       AND COALESCE(last_retry_at, created_at, updated_at) < ?
507:   `).run(nowIso, oldestAllowedIso).changes;
508: 
509:   const rows = db.prepare(`
510:     SELECT id FROM memory_index
511:     WHERE embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
512:       AND (embedding_status = 'retry' OR COALESCE(retry_count, 0) > 0)
513:     ORDER BY COALESCE(last_retry_at, created_at, updated_at) ASC, id ASC
514:     LIMIT -1 OFFSET ?
515:   `).all(maxPending) as Array<{ id: number }>;
516: 
517:   let overflow = 0;
518:   if (rows.length > 0) {
519:     const updateOverflow = db.prepare(`
520:       UPDATE memory_index
521:       SET embedding_status = 'failed',
522:           failure_reason = 'Retry retention pending cap exceeded',
523:           updated_at = ?
524:       WHERE id = ?
525:         AND embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
526:         AND (embedding_status = 'retry' OR COALESCE(retry_count, 0) > 0)
527:     `);
528:     const txn = db.transaction((ids: number[]) => {
529:       for (const id of ids) {
530:         overflow += updateOverflow.run(nowIso, id).changes;
531:       }
532:     });
533:     txn(rows.map((row) => row.id));
534:   }
535: 
536:   if (expired > 0 || overflow > 0) {
537:     console.warn(`[retry-manager] Retention pruned expired=${expired}, overflow=${overflow}`);
538:   }
539: 
540:   return { expired, overflow };
541: }

543: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
544:    3. RETRY QUEUE
545: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
546: 
547: function getRetryQueue(limit = 10): RetryMemoryRow[] {
548:   vectorIndex.initializeDb();
549:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
550:   if (!db) {
551:     console.warn('[retry-manager] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up.');
552:     return [];
553:   }
554:   enforceRetryRetentionLimits();
555:   const now = Date.now();
556: 
557:   const rows = db.prepare(`
558:     SELECT * FROM memory_index
559:     WHERE embedding_status IN ('pending', 'retry')
560:       AND retry_count < ?
561:     ORDER BY
562:       CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'pending' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
563:       retry_count ASC,
564:       created_at ASC
565:     LIMIT ?
566:   `).all(MAX_RETRIES, limit * 2) as RetryMemoryRow[];
567: 
568:   const eligible: RetryMemoryRow[] = [];
569:   for (const row of rows) {
570:     if (isEligibleForRetry(row, now)) {
571:       eligible.push(parseRow(row));
572:       if (eligible.length >= limit) break;
573:     }
574:   }
575: 
576:   return eligible;
577: }
578: 
579: function isEligibleForRetry(row: RetryMemoryRow, now: number): boolean {
580:   // Note: better-sqlite3 returns snake_case column names from SELECT *
581:   if (row.embedding_status === 'pending') return true;
582: 
583:   if (row.embedding_status === 'retry' && row.last_retry_at) {
584:     const lastRetry = new Date(row.last_retry_at).getTime();
585:     const retryCount = (row.retry_count as number) ?? (row.retryCount as number) ?? 0;
586:     // Off-by-one fix: retryCount is already incremented after the failure that triggered
587:     // The retry status, so use (retryCount - 1) for the backoff index. First retry (retryCount=1)
588:     // Should use BACKOFF_DELAYS[0] (1 minute), not BACKOFF_DELAYS[1] (5 minutes).
589:     const backoffIndex = Math.max(0, retryCount - 1);
590:     const requiredDelay = BACKOFF_DELAYS[Math.min(backoffIndex, BACKOFF_DELAYS.length - 1)];
591:     return (now - lastRetry) >= requiredDelay;
592:   }
593: 
594:   return row.embedding_status === 'retry';
595: }
596: 
597: function getFailedEmbeddings(): RetryMemoryRow[] {
598:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
599:   if (!db) {
600:     console.warn('[retry-manager] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up. Returning empty array.');
601:     return [];
602:   }
603: 
604:   const rows = db.prepare(`
605:     SELECT * FROM memory_index
606:     WHERE embedding_status = 'failed'
607:     ORDER BY updated_at DESC
608:   `).all() as RetryMemoryRow[];
609: 
610:   return rows.map(parseRow);
611: }
612: 
613: function getRetryStats(): RetryStats {
614:   const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
615:   if (!db) {
616:     console.warn('[retry-manager] Database not initialized. Server may still be starting up. Returning default stats.');
617:     return { pending: 0, retry: 0, failed: 0, success: 0, total: 0, queue_size: 0 };
618:   }
619: 
620:   const stats = db.prepare(`
621:     SELECT
622:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as pending,
623:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'retry' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as retry,
624:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failed,
625:       SUM(CASE WHEN embedding_status = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as success,
626:       COUNT(*) as total
627:     FROM memory_index
628:   `).get() as Record<string, number>;
629: 
630:   const result = {
631:     pending: stats.pending || 0,
632:     retry: stats.retry || 0,
633:     failed: stats.failed || 0,
634:     success: stats.success || 0,
635:     total: stats.total || 0,
636:     queue_size: (stats.pending || 0) + (stats.retry || 0),
637:   };
638: 
639:   refreshRetryHealthSnapshot(result);
640:   return result;
641: }
642: 
643: /**
644:  * Return a lightweight in-memory snapshot of embedding retry health.
645:  * PULL accessor — synchronous, zero DB access, always safe to call.
646:  * Returns zero-state when the retry manager has not yet been initialized.
647:  */
648: function getEmbeddingRetryStats(): EmbeddingRetryStats {
649:   return {
650:     pending: retryHealthSnapshot.pending,
651:     failed: retryHealthSnapshot.failed,
652:     retryAttempts: totalRetryAttempts,
653:     circuitBreakerOpen: isProviderCircuitOpen(),
654:     lastRun: lastBackgroundRunAt,
655:     queueDepth: retryHealthSnapshot.queueDepth,
656:   };
657: }
